    People also ask
    Is it cheaper to order from Grubhub or DoorDash?
    An AI Overview is not available for this searchCan't generate an AI overview right now. Try again later.
    AI Overview
    Grubhub is generally cheaper for large orders (over
    ), while DoorDash is usually cheaper for small, individual orders under
    . Grubhub often has lower service fees and, in some cases, lower menu prices, but the cheapest option varies significantly by city, restaurant, and current promotions, according to reports. 
    Key Takeaways for Cost Comparison: 
    • Large Orders (
      ):       Grubhub tends to be more affordable.
    • Small Orders (<
      ):       DoorDash is often cheaper.
    • Subscriptions: Both offer subscription services (Grubhub+ and DashPass) that waive delivery fees and lower service fees on eligible orders (
      00
       delivery fees on Grubhub+ and
      DashPass).
    • Hidden Costs: Both services often have higher menu prices than the restaurant, with one study showing Grubhub having higher average menu markups (66%) compared to DoorDash (59%).
    • Pro Tip: Amazon Prime members can get a free Grubhub+ membership. 
    Regional Variations:
    Research from NetCredit indicated that Grubhub was cheaper in 24 states, while DoorDash and Uber Eats were cheaper in 13 states each. DoorDash is often cheaper in major cities.

      Why did Grubhub lose to DoorDash?
      Grubhub's Struggles: What Went Wrong? Grubhub was an early pioneer in the U.S. food delivery market, but its dominance faded as competitors like DoorDash and Uber Eats capitalized on superior logistics, user interfaces, and aggressive expansion.
